item.title: Rod Liddle, 11th April 2026
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: On Times Radio, Claire joined academic Lisa McKenzie and presenter Rod Liddle to review the stories of the week. They discussed the prospect of a peace deal in the Iran war, the legacy of the Artemis moon mission, the Premier League With Pride campaign, fuel crisis protests in Ireland, changes to eligibility for council housing, ... Read more
item.content:encoded:
On Times Radio, Claire joined academic Lisa McKenzie and presenter Rod Liddle to review the stories of the week.

They discussed the prospect of a peace deal in the Iran war, the legacy of the Artemis moon mission, the Premier League With Pride campaign, fuel crisis protests in Ireland, changes to eligibility for council housing, the Green council election win in Thanet, and the denial of access to the UK for Kanye West in the name of ‘the public good’.

Listen to the whole programme on Times Radio’s website.

item.title: Women’s rights one year on from the Supreme Court ruling
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: Claire spoke to podcaster Penny Dee about the Supreme Court ruling on sex and single sex spaces – the gap between legal clarity and real world implementation, and why so many institutions still appear reluctant to act.
item.content:encoded: Claire spoke to podcaster Penny Dee about the Supreme Court ruling on sex and single sex spaces – the gap between legal clarity and real world implementation, and why so many institutions still appear reluctant to act.

item.title: Lords vote to scrap non-crime hate incidents
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: Claire spoke to TalkTV‘s Kevin O’Sullivan about the defeat of an attempt to continue the recording of non-crime hate incidents.
item.content:encoded: Claire spoke to TalkTV‘s Kevin O’Sullivan about the defeat of an attempt to continue the recording of non-crime hate incidents.

item.title: Free speech on life support
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: In conversation with Giuseppe Gracia of Schweizer Monat, the Swiss ‘magazine for doers and thinkers’, Claire discussed the fragile state of free speech in the UK, pressured by expanding hate and harm laws and a culture that treats offence as injury.
item.content:encoded: In conversation with Giuseppe Gracia of Schweizer Monat, the Swiss ‘magazine for doers and thinkers’, Claire discussed the fragile state of free speech in the UK, pressured by expanding hate and harm laws and a culture that treats offence as injury.

item.title: Claire on the DM-MI podcast
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: Claire appeared on the DM-MI podcast, where you can find conversations around Technology & Business, as well as passionate discussions on a range of topics from Mixed Martial Arts to History, Geo-Politics and Current Affairs. Speaking with host Sukh Hayre, Claire shared her unconventional political journey from revolutionary left activism to the House of Lords, ... Read more
item.content:encoded:
Claire appeared on the DM-MI podcast, where you can find conversations around Technology & Business, as well as passionate discussions on a range of topics from Mixed Martial Arts to History, Geo-Politics and Current Affairs.

Speaking with host Sukh Hayre, Claire shared her unconventional political journey from revolutionary left activism to the House of Lords, exploring the core principles that have shaped her views on sovereignty, democracy and Brexit.

The conversation also examined the current state of free speech in the UK, the impact of legislation such as the Online Safety Act, and the growing tension between cultural norms and political regulation.

Finally, they discussed the evolving UK political landscape and the rise of new political movements.

item.title: TalkTV, 17th February 2026
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: Claire spoke to Julia Hartley-Brewer about compensation for ‘wrongful’ phone seizures by border agents, the Filipino migrant who was given sex change treatments on the NHS, the offer of ‘US refuge’ for the British man who burnt a Koran, and revelations about Labour’s reaction to a corruption dossier.
item.content:encoded: Claire spoke to Julia Hartley-Brewer about compensation for ‘wrongful’ phone seizures by border agents, the Filipino migrant who was given sex change treatments on the NHS, the offer of ‘US refuge’ for the British man who burnt a Koran, and revelations about Labour’s reaction to a corruption dossier.

item.title: The Cabal Play
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: On The Mike Graham Show, Baroness Claire Fox delivered a blistering assessment of Keir Starmer’s leadership crisis, Labour’s internal chaos, and a political class increasingly detached from the voters it claims to represent.
item.content:encoded: On The Mike Graham Show, Baroness Claire Fox delivered a blistering assessment of Keir Starmer’s leadership crisis, Labour’s internal chaos, and a political class increasingly detached from the voters it claims to represent.

item.title: Starmer and Mandelson
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: “I almost don’t want him to resign over this… there’s so many reasons why he’s not fit to govern…” Claire spoke to TalkTV‘s Julia Hartley-Brewer about Keir Starmer’s handling of the Peter Mandelson scandal.
item.content:encoded:
“I almost don’t want him to resign over this… there’s so many reasons why he’s not fit to govern…”

Claire spoke to TalkTV‘s Julia Hartley-Brewer about Keir Starmer’s handling of the Peter Mandelson scandal.

item.title: Newsnight, 29th January 2026
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: Claire was a guest on Newsnight. Watch the discussion on iPlayer here: China charmer Starmer?
item.content:encoded: Claire was a guest on Newsnight. Watch the discussion on iPlayer here: China charmer Starmer?

item.title: Times Radio review of the week, 17th January 2026
item.dc:creator: Harley Richardson
item.description: Claire, Trevor Phillips and presenter Rod Liddle discussed the latest Tory defections to Reform, a school’s cancellation of a visit from the local Labour MP who was a member of Labour Friends of Israel, Trump’s intervention into the Iranian uprising, the retirement of the West Midlands Police for misleading MPs over the Maccabi football ban, ... Read more
item.content:encoded: Claire, Trevor Phillips and presenter Rod Liddle discussed the latest Tory defections to Reform, a school’s cancellation of a visit from the local Labour MP who was a member of Labour Friends of Israel, Trump’s intervention into the Iranian uprising, the retirement of the West Midlands Police for misleading MPs over the Maccabi football ban, the Darlington nurses’ victory in their employment tribunal case, proposed delays to some local council elections, the Labour MP’s bill to make it illegal for politicians to lie, and the mysterious hit singer Sienna Rose who may be an AI creation.
